title: Statistical inference & sufficiency
summary: A measure-theoretic foundation for sufficient, ancillary, and complete statistics, culminating in the Lehmann-Scheffé and Basu theorems of classical estimation theory.

theorems.name: Lehmann-Scheffé theorem
theorems.blurb: If T is a complete sufficient statistic and δ is unbiased for g(θ), then a function of T is the unique uniformly minimum-variance unbiased estimator (UMVUE) of g(θ).

theorems.name: Basu's theorem
theorems.latex: T \text{ complete sufficient},\ V \text{ ancillary} \implies T \perp\!\!\!\perp V \text{ under each } P_i
theorems.blurb: A boundedly complete sufficient statistic is independent of every ancillary statistic under each measure in the family.

theorems.name: Conditioning reduces MSE
theorems.blurb: The Rao-Blackwell L² projection step: replacing an estimator by its conditional expectation given a sub-σ-algebra never increases mean squared error.

theorems.name: Completeness uniqueness of unbiased estimators
theorems.latex: T \text{ complete},\ \mathbb{E}_\theta[f_i\circ T]=g(\theta) \implies f_1\circ T \stackrel{a.e.}{=} f_2\circ T
theorems.blurb: Under a complete statistic, any two unbiased σ(T)-measurable estimators of the same parameter coincide almost everywhere.

theorems.name: Sufficiency invariance of conditional expectation
theorems.blurb: If T is sufficient, the conditional expectation given σ(T) does not depend on the parameter θ.

theorems.name: Parametric family and sufficiency vocabulary
theorems.blurb: The headline structure modeling a parametric family of probability measures, underpinning the definitions of sufficient, complete, ancillary, and unbiased statistics.
